What it means: The champion's ability damage scales on Attack Damage.
Used by: Garen, Samira, Kled, Darius, Sion, Warwick, Caitlyn, Jhin, Twitch, Yasuo, Olaf

What it means: The champion's ability damage scales on Ability Power.
Used by: Kayle, Poppy, Galio, Sona, Jarvan IV, Lux, Taric, Maokai, Orianna, Amumu, Neeko

What it means: Neither. The champion's damage scales on some OTHER quantity, so the question has no answer for it.
Used by: Cho'Gath (12% of the TARGET's max HP), Ryze (150/200/300% of his own Armor + MR)

Damage Type: Split out of `Role` on 2026-07-21. Set 10's source glues the two together (`ADTank`, `APCaster`, `ADCarryCrit`), so normalising Role onto five nouns would have thrown this away — it is the only place either set states a champion's damage type. Set 9's source never stated it at all.

THE TWO SETS' VALUES HAVE DIFFERENT PROVENANCE. Set 10 is STATED: the prefix was read straight off the source's own role string, no inference. Set 9 is DERIVED: it is the stat the champion's ABILITY DAMAGE scales on, read off the `Amount` of its `Attack` effect rows.

UTILITY SCALING IS DELIBERATELY IGNORED. A shield or heal that happens to scale on AP does not make a champion a mage — that distinction is the whole rule. Warwick's only AP number is a 30/35/40% AP heal and Kled's is an Attack Speed buff; both deal their damage with auto-attacks, so both are AD.

THE DERIVATION WAS TESTED BEFORE IT WAS TRUSTED. Run against Set 10, where the answer is already stated, it agreed on 59 of 60. The single miss is Olaf — and he misses because his ability deals NO scaled damage, so the rule had nothing to read and fell through to a utility number. That is why the seven Set 9 champions in the same position are hand calls (Orianna, Maokai, Taric AP; Kled, Warwick AD; Cho'Gath, Ryze neither) rather than being guessed by a fallback.

WHERE A CHAMPION SCALES ON BOTH, THE DAMAGE ROW WINS AND THE LARGER NUMBER TAKES IT, read at TIER 2 — tier 3 is a luxury players do not reach and it skews every comparison (Senna's beam is 235/250/2000% AD, so tier 3 alone would swamp her 200/275% AP).
`—` IS AN ANSWER HERE, NOT A GAP. Cho'Gath and Ryze scale on neither stat, and saying so is more useful than forcing them into a bucket they do not belong in.
